title: Bridge Essay: Resting-state EEG in schizophrenia: auditory verbal hallucinations are related to shortening of specific microstates.
concept: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

**Resting-state EEG in schizophrenia: auditory verbal hallucinations are related to shortening of specific microstates.** — Clin Neurophysiol (2011). DOI: 10.1016/j.clinph.2010.10.042. PMID: 21123110. OBJECTIVE: Abnormal perceptions and cognitions in schizophrenia might be related to abnormal resting states of the brain. Previous research found that a specific class (class D) of sub-second electroencephalography (EEG) microstates was shortened in schizophrenia. This shortening correlated with positive symptoms. We questioned if this reflected positive psychotic traits or present psychopathology. METHODS: Resting-state EEGs of frequently hallucinating patients, indicating on- and offset of hal
